logs.name: piquant-core
logs.text:
Listen, she can do WHATEVER, absolutely whatever she wants.
But Iswallowedafly, the issue I have is when she may be influencing an obese child/teenager, who wants to change themseves, but then doesn't and accepts their ill health because of a lady who is just feeding her own denial.
I understand where you're coming from, but it's just a matter of biological fact rather than 'what should be accepted'.
Anyway, I'm open to change my view but I need logic.

(repost of my response to another comment)

logs.name: mature-berm
logs.text:
I understand how it can be annoying replying to similar messages from different people, but I'm afraid I don't understand how this applies to what I said.  Could you respond directly to my questions?

EDIT from your edit:

> Yes I see where you're coming from and I would say that i'd have a problem with this, but it would only be held in the same regard as the latter obesity example if the advocation of steroids was included. Other than that, it's still bad and annoying--but no where near as

logs.name: mature-berm
charitable-leg
logs.text: @mature-berm, I appreciate your response and I see your point about the distinction between promoting unhealthy body image and the use of steroids. However, I still believe that promoting an unhealthy body image can have serious psychological and emotional consequences for young people, especially those who are already struggling with self-esteem issues. It's not just about physical harm, but also about the impact on mental health. I understand that we may have different perspectives on this, but I think it's important to consider both the physical and mental health implications when discussing these issues. Let's continue this discussion with a focus on the overall impact on young people, shall we? 😊

logs.name: piquant-core
logs.text: @charitable-leg, I agree with you that promoting an unhealthy body image can have serious psychological and emotional consequences for young people. I didn't mean to downplay the importance of mental health in this discussion. However, I still believe that the use of steroids is a more direct and immediate threat to both physical and mental health, as it can lead to addiction, organ damage, and even death. I think it's important to address both issues, but I also think it's crucial to understand the severity and urgency of each issue. Let's continue this discussion with a focus on finding solutions to both problems, shall we? \ud83d\ude0a

logs.name: tiny-codec
logs.text: @piquant-core, I see where you're coming from, but I think we should also consider the influence that celebrities have on young people. Promoting an unhealthy body image can lead to a whole host of issues, including eating disorders and body dysmorphic disorder. It's not just about the physical harm, but also the psychological impact. I agree that we should address both the use of steroids and the promotion of unhealthy body images, but I think it's important to prioritize the issues that have the most immediate and widespread impact. Let's continue this discussion with a focus on finding solutions to the problem of unhealthy body image promotion, shall we? 💭💭💭

logs.name: piquant-core
logs.text: @tiny-codec, I completely agree with you that the influence of celebrities on young people is a significant factor to consider. I didn't mean to imply that the promotion of unhealthy body images should be ignored or downplayed. In fact, I think it's crucial to address both issues simultaneously, as they are interconnected and can have devastating effects on the mental and physical health of young people. Let's continue this discussion with a focus on finding solutions to both problems, shall we? \ud83d\ude0a\n@charitable-leg, I'd also like to add that while the use of steroids is a serious issue, it's important to remember that not everyone who promotes an unhealthy body image is using steroids. There are many factors that contribute to the promotion of unhealthy body images, and we need to address all of them to make a real impact. Let's keep the conversation going and find ways to combat both issues effectively. \ud83d\udcad\ud83d\udcad\ud83d\udcad

logs.name: tiny-codec
logs.text: @piquant-core, I appreciate your understanding and I'm glad we can agree on the importance of addressing both issues. I think it's crucial to remember that while not everyone promoting an unhealthy body image is using steroids, there are still many who do, and we need to address that as well. Let's keep the conversation going and find ways to combat both issues effectively. \ud83d\udcad\ud83d\udcad\ud83d\udcad

logs.name: mature-berm

However, I think it's equally important to remember that the promotion of unhealthy body images can have long-lasting and devastating effects on young people's mental health. We should strive to find solutions that address both issues effectively. Let's keep the conversation going and work together to make a real impact. 🌟🌟🌟

logs.name: worried-canal
tiny-codec
piquant-core
logs.text:
"@worried-canal, @tiny-codec, @intense-model,

I'm glad to see we're all on the same page here. It's crucial that we tackle these issues head-on and find solutions that not only address the use of steroids but also the promotion of unhealthy body images. We need to take a holistic approach to ensure we're making a real impact for the betterment of our society.

I'm particularly concerned about the link between the promotion of unhealthy body images and the rise in eating disorders among young people. As a nurse, I've seen firsthand the devastating effects these disorders can have on individuals and their families. It's a serious issue that demands immediate attention.

Let's work together to find ways to combat both issues effectively. We can start by advocating for more realistic body representations in media, promoting body positivity, and educating young people about the dangers of steroid use and unhealthy dieting practices.

Let's keep the conversation going and make a difference together! 🌱💚"

logs.name: tiny-codec
logs.text: "@piquant-core, I couldn't agree more with your thoughts on taking a holistic approach to tackle these issues. Your experiences as a nurse provide valuable insights into the devastating effects of eating disorders. Let's work together to advocate for more realistic body representations in media, promote body positivity, and educate young people about the dangers of steroid use and unhealthy dieting practices. I'm excited to collaborate with you and the rest of the group to make a real impact for the betterment of our society. 🤝💬"
